findings.title: Path instability in selective manifest linting mode
findings.description: The script accepts --manifest as raw Path and prints manifest_path.relative_to(ROOT). A relative path like brainstorm/skill.json is not under absolute ROOT, so relative_to() raises ValueError, crashing the linter.
findings.fix: Normalize all manifest CLI inputs to repo-rooted absolute paths before validation/output by converting each to (ROOT / path).resolve() when relative.

findings.title: Validator crash on malformed uses.* entries
findings.description: The validate_manifest function tests array items for membership (e.g., in PROFILES). Non-string unhashable items like dicts or lists in uses.dispatch_profiles, uses.packet_builders, or uses.artifact_schemas will raise TypeError instead of returning a validation issue.
findings.fix: Validate that each array member is a non-empty string before performing membership checks.

findings.title: Review artifacts anchored to process CWD instead of project directory
findings.description: Review output directories are built using Path('.model-review/...') relative to the process CWD rather than the project directory resolved from --project. This causes artifacts to be written to the wrong tree if the script is invoked from outside the target repository.
findings.fix: Anchor review_dir creation to project_dir or add an explicit --output-dir argument.

findings.title: Brainstorm schema registry underspecifies documented artifact contract
findings.description: Registry entries for brainstorm.matrix.v1 and brainstorm.coverage.v1 require fewer fields than documented (missing transfer_mechanism, caller_evidence, etc.), allowing malformed artifacts to pass contract checks.
findings.fix: Expand ARTIFACT_SCHEMAS definitions to match the documented matrix/coverage requirements or update documentation to match the registry.

findings.title: Contradictory fallback policy in review documentation
findings.description: Documentation contains internal contradictions, stating both that models should NEVER be downgraded on failure while also documenting automatic Gemini Pro to Flash fallback behavior.
findings.fix: Align the documentation with actual implemented policy: automated fallback is allowed as implemented by the dispatch layer.
